sentence_reviews.text: A learner maps inputs to labeled targets using an explicit error signal.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples', including 19.1 'Forms of Learning', 19.2 'Supervised Learning', and 19.3 'Learning Decision Trees' (lines 488-508).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected AIMA table of contents directly identifies supervised learning as learning from examples; the sentence is a concise definition of that setup.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Supervised learning is modeled as depending on Datasets, sampling & splits, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences and feeding downstream nodes such as Semi-supervised & weak supervision, Preference learning, Active learning & curriculum learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
